scenarios.input: A cloud file storage vendor provides document management for legal contracts. Files stored on the platform are not encrypted at rest. Data is encrypted in transit via TLS but at-rest encryption is not implemented.
scenarios.notes: no_encryption=30. Total=30. Rating: Medium. Baseline single encryption failure — establishes that missing encryption alone is Medium, not High.

scenarios.input: A marketing analytics vendor processes customer behavioural data for campaign targeting. No data retention policy exists. Customer data is kept indefinitely with no deletion schedule or data lifecycle management.
scenarios.notes: no_data_retention=10. Total=10. Rating: Low. Tests that retention alone does not inflate to Medium. Contrast with DP-010 (retention + SOC2 = 25 = Medium).

scenarios.input: A customer support ticketing vendor stores support conversations including personal contact details and account information. The ticket database is not encrypted at rest. No data retention policy exists — tickets are retained permanently with no deletion schedule.
scenarios.notes: no_encryption=30, no_data_retention=10. Total=40. Rating: Medium. Two data protection failures remaining Medium. Both controls must be identified.

scenarios.input: A cloud database vendor stores financial transaction records for retail clients. Data at rest is not encrypted. Security scans have identified critical unpatched CVE vulnerabilities on the production database servers that expose stored data to exploitation.
scenarios.notes: no_encryption=30, critical_cve_exposure=30. Total=60. Rating: High. Data unprotected AND systems exploitable. Tests cross-domain source citation — two documents expected.

scenarios.input: A vendor managing employee health and wellness programme data does not encrypt personal health records at rest. The vendor has not conducted an annual penetration test — the last assessment was 22 months ago and no test is currently scheduled.
scenarios.notes: no_encryption=30, no_annual_pentest=20. Total=50. Rating: MEDIUM — exactly at the Medium ceiling. Any High response is a business accuracy failure. Second boundary scenario (IAM-010 was the first, different controls).

scenarios.input: A vendor providing cloud storage for intellectual property and product design documents cannot provide SOC2 Type II or ISO 27001 certification. Data stored on the platform is not encrypted at rest. The vendor stores proprietary engineering files and product roadmaps.
scenarios.notes: no_encryption=30, no_soc2_iso_evidence=15. Total=45. Rating: Medium. Cross-domain: data protection + compliance. Two distinct source documents expected.

scenarios.input: A vendor processing personal health information for insurance claims stores PHI without encryption at rest. The vendor has no documented incident response plan — in the event of a data breach there is no defined procedure for containment, notification, or remediation.
scenarios.notes: no_encryption=30, no_ir_plan=15. Total=45. Rating: Medium. Data vulnerability paired with no breach response capability. Incident Response Policy must be cited.

scenarios.input: An e-commerce analytics vendor stores customer purchase history, browsing behaviour, and payment card metadata. No encryption at rest on any storage layer. No data retention policy — all data kept indefinitely. The vendor cannot provide SOC2 Type II or ISO 27001 compliance documentation.
scenarios.notes: no_encryption=30, no_data_retention=10, no_soc2_iso_evidence=15. Total=55. Rating: High. Three failures cross into High. FM-005 test — all three controls must be identified. Three-source scenario.

scenarios.input: A vendor managing employee payroll and salary records for a 2,000-person organisation stores all compensation data unencrypted at rest. Critical CVE vulnerabilities on production servers remain unpatched for over 90 days. MFA is not enforced on administrator accounts that have direct database access.
scenarios.notes: mfa_missing=25, no_encryption=30, critical_cve_exposure=30. Total=85. Rating: Critical. Data category's Critical scenario. Unprotected sensitive data, exploitable systems, weak access control. Three source documents. FM-004 test — numerical hallucination check at Critical severity.

scenarios.input: A market research vendor collects survey responses including demographic data and health indicators from study participants. No data retention policy is in place — participant data is kept indefinitely. The vendor cannot provide SOC2 Type II or ISO 27001 documentation.
scenarios.notes: no_data_retention=10, no_soc2_iso_evidence=15. Total=25. Rating: Medium — first score in Medium territory (21-50). Contrast with DP-002 (score=10=Low) to validate boundary. Any Low response is a business accuracy failure.
